candidate: Likely contact dermatitis. Avoid suspected allergens.  Antihistamines may help. See a doctor if it worsens.
query_text: What is this?  Can you look into it quickly?  Urgent. It is itchy all over the body these few days.  Wherever I scratch, it would become itchy, and forming hives.  What is the problem?  I have this problem for all kinds of trouser belts.  Only these few days.
gold_texts: ["Based on the picture, it is urticaria.  Treatment: Yupingfeng granules (A traditional Chinese medicine that nourishes the bodiy and improves immunity), Desloratadine Citrate Disodium tablet.", "Urticaria.  Treatment with anti-allergic medicines should be OK.", "Based on the picture and medical history, it is acute urticaria.  If there is no improvement after taking anithistamine medication orally, better to get a routine blood test with dermatologist to see if the disease is casued by infection of any kind.", "Urticaria.  Treatment with anti-allergic medicine. Avoid working overnight.  Consume less alcohol.  Get a good balance between work and leisure.", "Acute Urticaria.  Need to actively seeking the origins or causes resulting in it and have them removed.  Should take anti-allergic drug as treatment at the same time, such as Loratadine tablet.  One tablet every day.", "Treatment with anti-allgergic medicine.", "How are you?  Your urticaria is caused by allergy to something.  Treatment: Don't use this leather belt any more.  Next take orally chlorphenamine maleate tablet, 3 times a day, 2 tablets each time.", "Urticaria.  Treatment with anti-allgergic medicine.", "If this happens occasionally, may use anti-allgergic drugs.  If it is recurrent, need to find out the allergens, and use of traditional chinese medicine orally."]

query_text: I had a burn from the day before yesterday afternoon. Now there is blister. I popped the blister this morning, as shown in the image. I spray the wound with polito now, is the wound inflamed or infected? The wound dose not hurt (minor pain when you press on it). The surrounding is slightly red
gold_texts: ["The redness around the wound is inflammation from the prior burn. The wound does not appear to be infected.", "The wound appears to be healing well on his own, if the skin opens again, I would recommend dressing the wound with bacitracin ointment and xeroform dressing. ￼"]

query_text: Help, I fell from a bike resulting in a wound like this. It has been two days, does it need stitches There was pus formation on the side. The wound is approximately 3-4mm deep. There was occasional bleeding for the past two days. Does it need stitches? If it does not need stitches, will it have hypertrophic scar after the wound heals?
gold_texts: ["Closing the wound at this time risks infection. Continue to cleanse with soap and water and apply dry dressing. Hypertrophic scar formation cannot be predicted at this time but you have a higher chance of developing hypertorphic scar if you have developed them in the past.", "Wound appears to be abrasion with exposed dermis.  Does not appear to be a laceration amenable to suture repair.  If the wound is greater than 24 hours old is not appropriate for suture repair.  To promote wound healing, clean the wound thoroughly with warm soapy water.  You may apply topical antibiotic ointment and cover with a nonadherent dressing.  Dressing may be changed once daily and during dressing changes wound may be cleaned gently with warm soapy water.  Avoid any drying agents such as alcohol, hydrogen peroxide, or iodine to open skin.  Tetanus should be updated if tetanus is not up-to-date.  Once the wound is healed, to minimize scar formation avoid UV exposure and use of scar minimization creams such as Mederma"]
